title: FrontLane Agent Group Container Config
description: Per-agent-group configuration at groups/<folder>/container.json. Generated from src/container-config.ts — run pnpm generate:container-schema to refresh.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor validation (not read by runtime).

a2aSessionMode.description:
Agent-to-agent worker session policy.
- `agent-shared`: one shared a2a session per target agent group
- `root-session`: one a2a session per target agent group and root session

agentGroupId.description: Agent group ID — set by the host, read by the runner.

assistantName.description: Assistant display name (used in system prompt / responses).

env.description: Optional env vars forwarded into the container at spawn time. Use this to point a skill's bridge.py at a non-default backend (e.g. `CAMERA_BASE_URL=http://172.18.198.229:8001`) without rebuilding the image or hard-coding hostnames in skill code. Keys/values passed verbatim via `docker run -e KEY=VALUE`. Provider/system env (TZ, OneCLI HTTPS_PROXY, etc.) is layered separately and is not overridden by this map.

groupName.description: Agent group display name (used in transcript archiving).

idleExitMs.description: Idle exit window in milliseconds. When > 0, the container poll loop exits cleanly after this long without trigger-eligible work. Read by the agent-runner; 0 keeps legacy behavior (host-sweep 30 min ceiling).

maxMessagesPerPrompt.description: Max messages per prompt. Falls back to code default if unset.

memoryMode.description:
Long-lived memory policy for this agent group.
- `workspace`: agent may persist durable notes in its shared workspace
- `erp`: durable user/business memory must go through the ERP gateway

progressiveDisclosure.description:
Progressive skill disclosure (perf). Replaces each skill's full `instructions.md` content in the composed system prompt to keep the prompt small and prefix-cache friendly.

  false (default)  — current behavior: every skill's instructions.md                      is inlined into the system prompt.   true             — emit a compact `Available Skills` index                      (name + description from SKILL.md frontmatter) +                      anti-overthink preamble telling the agent to                      skip `load_skill` for simple greetings. Use                      `load_skill(name)` MCP tool to fetch a skill on                      demand.   "lean"           — omit the skill index entirely. For dispatcher                      agents that route to worker agents and never                      need to execute skills themselves                      (`frontlane-frontdesk` pattern). The                      `load_skill` tool stays registered but the                      agent has no list to choose from.

Worker agent groups that already specify a narrow `skills` array usually leave this off — their prompt is already small.

resources.description: Optional per-container resource caps. Unset fields default to Docker's unlimited. Strongly recommended in multi-user deployments.

skills.description: Which skills to enable — array of skill names or "all" (default).

ContainerResourceLimits.description: Per-agent-group container resource limits. All fields are optional and map directly onto Docker run flags. When a field is unset the Docker default applies (unlimited). Strongly recommended in multi-tenant deployments — a single runaway agent can otherwise exhaust host memory or fork-bomb the kernel.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for the ERP gateway, without a trailing slash.

defaultHeaders.description: Static headers to send on every ERP gateway request.

signingHeaders.description: Overrides for the three signing header names. Leave unset to use the defaults above — only set this if the gateway you're fronting has mandatory header naming rules.

signingKey.description: Optional HMAC signing key. When set, every gateway request is signed: `x-frontlane-timestamp`, `x-frontlane-nonce`, and `x-frontlane-signature = HMAC-SHA256(key, timestamp + "." + nonce + "." + body)`. Gateways that validate signatures can then reject requests that didn't come from a host-provisioned container. Pair with a short clock-skew window (e.g. ±5 minutes) and a replay cache on the gateway side.

timeoutMs.description: Request timeout for built-in ERP gateway MCP tools. Default: 15000 ms.
